member.name: Calum Miller
member.synopsis: Calum Miller is the Liberal Democrat MP for Bicester and Woodstock , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Foreign Affairs).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: MOD Bicester: Asylum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether contracts have been awarded to companies to undertake assessments of Site A at MoD Bicester in preparation for using it as an asylum centre.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This is a matter for the Home Office, not the Ministry of Defence, as they have responsibility for setting up and running asylum accommodation on MOD sites.

writtenQuestions.heading: MOD Bicester: Asylum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether any service personnel, MoD staff or MoD contractors have been asked to sign non-disclosure agreements with respect to proposals to use MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No non-disclosure agreements have been signed by MOD staff, contractors or service personnel with respect to proposals to use MOD Bicester for asylum accommodation.

writtenQuestions.heading: MOD Bicester: Asylum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, when his Department was first informed of proposals to use MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence has been working with our colleagues in the Home Office to support the closure of asylum hotels for some time. The Ministry of Defence was first informed of proposals to use MOD Bicester for asylum accommodation in November 2025.

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether measures and mechanisms are in place for the new Maternity and Neonatal Commissioner to assess whether the views and concerns of women, families and frontline staff are being reflected in t
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has announced that we will appoint the first ever Maternity and Neonatal Commissioner. This was one of Baroness Amos’ urgent actions from her national maternity and neonatal investigation final report. We accept that this is a crucial first...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what powers and responsibilities the new Maternity and Neonatal Commissioner will have to improve accountability and organisational culture within NHS trusts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has announced that we will appoint the first ever Maternity and Neonatal Commissioner. This was one of Baroness Amos’ urgent actions from her national maternity and neonatal investigation final report. We accept that this is a crucial first...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what estimate her Department has made of the cost of preparing, operating and securing MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Due diligence and assurance work for all proposed large sites including Bicester is still ongoing. This includes assessing accurate costings. Once complete, business cases and approvals will be sought in the normal way and details published in the normal ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what discussions she has had with the Secretary of State for Defence regarding the proposed use of MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No decision has been made in respect of the use of MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.The Home Office has engaged regularly with the Secretary of State for Defence regarding the proposed use of the site. Suitability assessments are ongoing and will con...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment her Department has made of the accessibility of MoD Bicester to amenities, public transport, healthcare, legal advice and asylum support services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No decision has been made in respect of the use of MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.The Home Office has engaged regularly with the Secretary of State for Defence regarding the proposed use of the site. Suitability assessments are ongoing and will con...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what alternative sites were considered before MoD Bicester was selected for asylum accommodation and what criteria were used to compare those sites.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No decision has been made in respect of the use of MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.The Home Office has engaged regularly with the Secretary of State for Defence regarding the proposed use of the site. Suitability assessments are ongoing and will con...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether consultation was undertaken with Cherwell District Council, Oxfordshire County Council, Thames Valley Police and local NHS bodies prior to the decision to proceed with the use of MoD Bicester
writtenQuestions.answerText: No decision has been made in respect of the use of MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.The Home Office has engaged regularly with the Secretary of State for Defence regarding the proposed use of the site. Suitability assessments are ongoing and will con...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what criteria her Department used to assess the suitability of MoD Bicester for use as asylum accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No decision has been made in respect of the use of MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation. Suitability assessments are ongoing and will consider a range of factors including safety, security, operational deliverability, planning requirements, and the impac...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment her Department has made of the adequacy of previous proposals to develop MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation; and for what reason those proposals were not taken forward.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Previous proposals relating to MoD Bicester were considered under a previous government as part of work to identify potential asylum accommodation options.They were not taken forward following assessments of operational deliverability which were relevant ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if she will publish any site assessment, risk assessment, equality impact assessment and value-for-money assessment prepared in relation to the proposed use of MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office undertakes appropriate due diligence when considering potential asylum accommodation sites, including assessment of suitability, deliverability, safety, and planning. We do not routinely publish internal assessments relating to sites. Cost...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of housing asylum seekers at MoD Bicester on local healthcare services, policing, transport infrastructure, community safety and volunta
writtenQuestions.answerText: No decision has been made in respect of the use of MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation. Suitability assessments are ongoing and will consider a range of factors including safety, security, operational deliverability, and planning requirements and the im...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: MOD Bicester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether a formal review has been undertaken of previous proposals to use MoD Bicester for asylum accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office keeps the asylum accommodation estate under regular review, including learning from previous proposals and existing accommodation sites.

writtenQuestions.heading: Campsfield Immigration Removal Centre
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the impact of 240 additional detention beds at Campsfield Immigration Removal Centre on the number of enforced returns.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The additional 240 beds at Campsfield Immigration Removal Centre (IRC) form part of the IRC Expansion Programme, which will deliver around 1,000 additional long‑term male bed spaces, increasing overall detention capacity to approximately 3,500 beds.Increa...

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Police Custody
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many referrals to Liaison and Diversion services from police custody were received only after the person referred had been released from custody in each of the last two years for which data is
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested is not held as NHS England does not capture this specific data on referrals. However, it expects this will happen on a regular basis as Liaison and Diversion teams are, on average, only present within police custody suites for 12...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Police Custody
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what measures are in place to prevent a person detained in police custody who has been identified as requiring an assessment by Liaison and Diversion services from being given an out of court disposal
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office is unable to comment on operational police matters and decisions as policing is independent of the government. Guidance is provided to forces through the College of Policings Approved Professional Practice (APP) which includes information ...
